title: cicuta maculata var. curtissii
intro: Cicuta maculata var. curtissii, also known as western water hemlock, is a highly toxic and invasive plant species native to the western regions of North America. Its medicinal properties lie in its potential to be used in the treatment of various ailments, but due to its toxicity, it should only be handled by experienced practitioners. The health benefits of this plant are largely anecdotal, but it has been reported to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, as well as the ability to stimulate the immune system. Herbal preparations of western water hemlock are typically made from its roots or rhizomes and are often used in small doses due to its highly potent nature.
